quotes.text: The phrase "saith within my heart" is a Hebrew idiom meaning "it seems to me.
quotes.speaker: Ellis T. Rasmussen
quotes.source: A Latter-day Saint Commentary on the Old Testament
quotes.attr: Ellis T. Rasmussen, A Latter-day Saint Commentary on the Old Testament, 425

quotes.text: In the first place they were on the same standing with their brethren. In the beginning all spirits were created equal. There were none more privileged than were others. All were nurtured at the side of our Heavenly Father. All were blessed with the capability of learning. Heaven gave them love, and they, themselves, developed understanding. Wisdom came with understanding, while knowledge preceded them both. Knowledge, quickly followed by understanding, and then by wisdom, made strong the just and true. To the just and true, strength was added upon strength, justice upon justice, faith upon faith. The strong became stronger. As the strong became stronger, the Light of Christ illumined their way and in it they saw the reality of His Atonement. We are here reminded of certain words of Isaiah: "O House of Jacob, come ye and let us walk in the Light of the Lord." (II Nephi 12:5). For, said David, the Psalmist, "In Thy Light shall we see light," (Psalm 36:9). We cannot refrain from quoting the Sweet Singer of Israel as he continued his song of praise to God because what he expressed therein is just what the Holy Father did with His faithful children both here and before they came here: "O continue Thy loving kindness unto them that know Thee; and Thy righteousness to the upright in heart.
quotes.speaker: George Reynolds and Janne M. Sjodahl
quotes.source: Commentary on the Book of Mormon
quotes.attr: George Reynolds and Janne M. Sjodahl, Commentary on the Book of Mormon, edited and arranged by Philip C. Reynolds, 7 vols., 3:, p.201
